main_ingredients: flavourings (e.g. red bean, pandan, yam, durian)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Potong ice cream bar weighs approximately 60 g and delivers about 83 kcal, 10.5 g carbohydrate, and 7.8 g sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Potong Ice Cream — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance at confidence 0.95 for all per-100g fields. Values are accepted as-is from the HPB Foodo database entry for Potong ice cream.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — ice cream / frozen dairy-free dessert analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Potong ice cream exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI 57 (medium) is adopted from the input record (confidence 0.844) and is consistent with published GI values for coconut-milk-based frozen desserts and regular ice cream (GI range 37–62, mean ~51–57) reported in Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Glycemic load per 60 g serving = (57 × 10.5 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 6.0. This is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended for definitive classification.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (12.93 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (1.6 g/100g) triggers insulin-mediated sodium retention despite very low intrinsic sodium (21 mg/100g); coconut-milk saturated fat (~5.8 g/100g) contributes to arterial stiffness.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Potong ice cream to one bar (≈60 g) on an occasional basis, as the high sugar load drives insulin-mediated sodium retention and the saturated fat content from coconut milk may worsen arterial stiffness over time.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Total carbohydrate 17.49 g/100g with sugar 12.93 g/100g and only 1.6 g/100g dietary fibre produces rapid postprandial glucose elevation; GI 57 (medium) with low fibre buffering worsens glycaemic impact; coconut-milk saturated fat exacerbates insulin resistance.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat Potong ice cream as an occasional indulgence only — one bar (~60 g) delivers approximately 7.8 g sugar and 10.5 g total carbohydrate — and should monitor postprandial glucose closely, pairing with a high-fibre meal context where possible.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~5.8 g/100g from coconut milk (approximately 86% of total fat) raises LDL cholesterol risk; high sugar (12.93 g/100g) promotes hypertriglyceridaemia and endothelial inflammation with regular consumption.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Potong ice cream to occasional small portions, as the high coconut-milk saturated fat content raises LDL cholesterol and the high sugar load promotes triglyceride elevation and vascular inflammation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Coconut-milk saturated fat (~5.8 g/100g) exceeds the RA pro-inflammatory threshold and is explicitly excluded from the MUFA/PUFA carve-out; sugar at 12.93 g/100g further promotes inflammatory cytokine production (TNF-alpha, IL-6).
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid regular consumption of Potong ice cream, as both the coconut-milk saturated fat and high sugar content independently promote systemic inflammation and may worsen joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Potong ice cream (Malay: Aiskrim Potong; Chinese: 波东雪糕) is a traditional Southeast Asian frozen dessert bar moulded on a flat wooden stick. The name 'potong' means 'cut' in Malay, referring to the sliced or moulded block format. It is a beloved street food and nostalgic treat in Singapore, Malaysia, and Indonesia, sold by bicycle vendors and hawker stalls. The base is typically made from coconut milk, sugar, and corn flour, giving it a dense, creamy, and slightly chewy texture that distinguishes it from Western-style dairy ice cream. Common flavours include red bean (azuki), pandan, yam (taro), durian, and sweet corn. Per 100 g, Potong ice cream provides 138 kcal, 1.7 g protein, 6.76 g fat (predominantly saturated fat ~5.8 g from coconut milk), 17.49 g carbohydrate, 12.93 g sugar, 1.6 g dietary fibre, and only 21 mg sodium. It is dairy-free, halal, and suitable for vegans. Clinically, the high sugar content (12.93 g/100g) with low fibre (1.6 g/100g) is a concern for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes and for insulin-mediated sodium retention in hypertension. The coconut-milk saturated fat load is relevant for cardiovascular disease and rheumatoid arthritis management. One standard bar (~60 g) delivers approximately 83 kcal and 7.8 g sugar. GI is estimated at 57 (medium), yielding a glycaemic load of approximately 6 per serving.
common_user_queries: Is potong ice cream dairy free?
Can diabetics eat potong ice cream?
What flavours does potong ice cream come in?

confidence_reason: All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95. The food is an exact match in the HPB database. Saturated fat is estimated from coconut-milk composition given HPB does not separately report fatty acid fractions for this entry. GI is an analogous estimate consistent with published coconut-milk frozen dessert GI ranges.
notes: 1. saturated_fat_g (5.8 g/100g) is estimated from coconut-milk composition (coconut milk saturated fat ~85–90% of total fat; 6.76 g fat × 0.86 ≈ 5.8 g); HPB Foodo does not separately report fatty acid fractions for this entry. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for Potong ice cream are not separately reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 3. GI value of 57 is an analogous estimate based on coconut-milk frozen dessert analogues from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021; direct measurement for Potong ice cream is not available. Direct GI testing is recommended for definitive classification. 4. Dietary flags: Potong ice cream is typically dairy-free (coconut milk base), halal, and vegan, but flavour variants may differ — consumers should verify product labels. 5. Allergen note: coconut is classified as a tree nut allergen in some jurisdictions (e.g. US FDA); consumers with tree nut allergies should exercise caution.
